output:
CVE-2020-16024 refers to a heap buffer overflow vulnerability found in the UI component of Google Chrome versions prior to 87.0.4280.66. This vulnerability allows a remote attacker, having already compromised the renderer process, to achieve a potential sandbox escape through a specially crafted HTML page.

The vulnerability is classified under CWE-787, which denotes improper handling of buffer boundaries - specifically writing data beyond the intended buffer limits. This type of flaw can lead to a variety of impacts including unauthorized access and data corruption.

This is a critical severity vulnerability as it may provide a pathway for remote code execution (RCE) leading to a compromise of the browser sandbox, widely utilized by malicious actors as categorized under MITRE ATT&CK Technique T1203. Affected software includes Google Chrome versions before 87.0.4280.66 on various platforms.

Mitigation strategies include ensuring all systems are updated to the latest secure versions, and applying measures such as application whitelisting, monitoring and modifying behaviors that deviate from normal activity patterns, and employing strong sandboxing techniques.
